notes: This is a tough one to rate because the game itself runs perfectly out of the box. I clicked 'Play' and it booted up no worries at all. I fiddled around in settings, changing graphics and key configs for a while and then spent a good chunk of time running around in the firing range. There was a tiny bit of lag when I first loaded in as the textures were catching up, but once that was done it was smooth sailing. No problems with sound at all either. I used a variety of guns and spent some time working through the CQC area. I did not have a single issue the entire time. However, and it's a big however, I was kicked immediately from every server that I tried to join. EAC boots straight away and gives the 'dummy client' reason. The game itself scores a Platinum rating because it ran without any issue whatsoever. The Borked rating is wholly and solely because of EAC and, being that Squad is a multiplayer only game, it effectively makes the game crucially unplayable. As previously stated, the training mode works perfectly well, but who is going to want to spend more than 15min or so firing at targets? I wasn't able to find any non-EAC servers to test the ability to join.
title: Squad

notes: Game loads and runs perfectly, however due to the use of Easy Anti-Cheat (EAC) connecting to any server is impossible. The game attempts to connect and may even begin to load the team selection, however you'll immediately be booted back to the main menu.

Multiplayer/EAC works good.

notes: Renaming the movies folder got the game past the initial black screen crash that was introduced in the new v13. Game ran with acceptable performance but after a few hours of play particle effect textures and vehicle geometry get corrupted. Restarting the game fixes this issue, but random crashes seemed to ramp up the more I played as well. This may be related to dxvk shader caching as the crashes stop for a few hours after deleting the shader cache.

notes: I had to rename the video folder as others have described, but it was platinum performance other than that. Go here "/steam/steamapps/common/Squad/SquadGame/Content/Movies" and change the folder name to something other than "Movies" (eg MoviesOld) and you're good to go. I'm running at mostly high settings in 1440p and generally hovering around 40-50fps, which is comparable to my Windows performance. One extremely happy Linux gamer right here ^-^
title: Squad

notes: Alpha 13.1 patch released today and from the changelog - "Fixed an engine crash that happened if required files to play the introduction movie were missing." It is no longer necessary to change the name of the Movies folder. Instead of playing an intro, there will be a momentary black screen and then you will reach the menu screen. This game no longer requires any tweaking whatsoever to play, multiplayer continues to work and performance (for me) is very good. It now plays perfectly out of the box.

notes: I tested many combinations of settings and the game mostly runs as good as it did on Windows 10. Supersampling at 1.25 (as I had it set in Windows) seems to cause stuttering and frequent crashes. Could be my hardware as I haven't seen it listed elsewhere. The only thing keeping this game from platinum is an infrequent crash with no debugging information at all - it usually happens when a shot is fired and will cause the game to freeze with only background music playing for maybe 20 seconds before the game window closes. This happens maybe once every three games. Absolutely playable and these crashes are possibly resolved with tweaks, though I am not experienced enough with wine to go digging too far with the very limited information I have regarding the crash.
title: Squad

notes: The game will crash intermittently, however it is generally a crash that you will be able to predict due to various in-explainable circumstances. Most games that you first join will not crash. Big maps like Skorpo and Narva seem to do a bit worse in terms of performance as the same performance hits that make Windows users suffer on those maps seem to affect Proton users, making the game crash sometimes.

notes: This game runs perfectly fine for me except that the game will not load unless it is made full-screen for start-up. After being in the game, I can minimize and make it windowed/not full-screen. However, it will crash to desktop if it is not started as full-screen. I would rate this platinum because that also seems to be an error for Windows Users. I know I had it for Win 10 (see https://steamcommunity.com/app/393380/discussions/0/135512931360000834/) so I am not sure this is a Linux specific issue vs Squad launcher issue. I was able in KDE to tell it to launch full-screen and have not had a single issue. I have more than 10 hours so far with long sessions played.
